title: Decide on best gaming laptop — pre-camp prep todo
html:
TL;DR

  • July 24 todo dump: pick a gaming laptop, get the concept for the app, find a way to produce money (Craigslist, eBay, streets), get a music playlist, start outline for college question during camp.
  • Sits inside the YES Kennedy Lugar / CIEE-orbit "camp" prep — the "college question during camp" line places this just before a residential summer program where Yahir expected to be asked the "why college / why this school" prompt.
  • Names "the app" with no antecedent — earliest extant reference to an unspecified Yahir-built app in the 2019 corpus, predating any concrete product idea by 2+ years. Goes into the dream-app germination lineage that culminates in stealth-apps.
  • "Find a way to produce money — Craigslist, eBay, streets, etc" is the first time he writes down the bridge income verb-string at age 17 — "streets" deserves attention as a candid scope-marker.
  • Same 4-day arc as talk about the circle (Jul 18), tile for eye for Alexandria (Jul 19), situations with girls after Alexandria (Jul 20) — the Alexandria departure week, plus prep for whatever "camp" came next.

Notes

The note is an itinerary-fragment — five bullets, no prose, but the ordering tells a story: hardware (laptop) → product (app concept) → revenue (money) → media (playlist) → identity-rehearsal (college-question outline). It's a startup-OPS template applied to a 17-year-old's summer life.

The "app" mention is striking because Yahir doesn't yet specify what it does. By the time the corpus is dense again (Outpace, Swipr, Vrtl Bet) the app is the central productive form. This 2019 line is the earliest extant generic-app-as-vehicle mention — the placeholder before the placeholder gets filled.

"Streets" in the money-producing list is the most interesting word. Yahir is in Grants Pass, a 39k-population town with no real "streets" hustle ecosystem in the NYC sense — so "streets" reads as either teenage rhetorical mimicry or a literal door-to-door flag. Either way it's the candid version of bridge income that Brenda or Ford paperwork would never see.

"College question during camp" confirms this is residential-summer-program prep. The 2019 Ford / YES / FBLA camp orbit had Yahir at multiple programs.

Open questions

  • Which camp specifically? The exact program isn't named.
  • Did the laptop ever get bought? No follow-up purchase in the surfaced corpus.
  • "The concept for the app" — first dream-app mention. What was it? Likely undefined; the verb is more important than the noun here.

Connections

  • 2019 07 18 talk about the circle last night and how she felt · 2019 07 19 get the tile ready for eye for alexandria · 2019 07 20 figure out situations with girls after alexandria — same week
  • · dream app — the app concept lineage
  • bridge income strategies · grants pass teen era · yes kennedy lugar · ciee · ford family foundation · fbla
